title: Phanpy (web) full compat pass
description:
## What
Log into Phanpy (https://phanpy.social) pointed at local smallhold. Exercise the following flows and document pass/fail for each: login via OAuth, home timeline load, compose + post, reply to a post, boost, like/unlike, media upload and attachment, notifications panel, search, profile edit. Fix every broken flow. Phanpy is the minimum release bar — all flows must be green.

## Why
Phanpy is the simplest well-maintained web client. It makes a predictable set of API calls and is the easiest to debug in a browser. If Phanpy doesn't work, the release is blocked regardless of any other pass/fail.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: open Phanpy's source (https://github.com/cheeaun/phanpy) and enumerate every API endpoint it calls during: OAuth dance, timeline load, compose, reply, boost, like, media upload, notifications, search, profile edit; note any endpoints with unusual parameter shapes or response requirements.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: for each broken flow discovered during testing, identify the failing endpoint and response shape; fix the handler to match Phanpy's expectation (using mastodon.social as oracle for the correct shape); ensure Idempotency-Key on POST /statuses is handled.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests for each Phanpy-critical endpoint asserting the exact JSON shape (field names, types, presence of required fields); use mastodon.social's actual response as the oracle shape.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social API responses for each endpoint, captured via curl; Phanpy source code as ground truth for what fields it reads.
- Test approach: integration tests asserting exact JSON shape for each endpoint; browser smoke test against local instance for final acceptance.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths.
- `id` field must always be a string (not a number) — Phanpy parses it as a string.
- `media_attachments`, `mentions`, `tags`, `emojis` must be arrays, never null.
- `in_reply_to_id` must be string or null — never empty string or absent.
- Pagination `Link` header must use `rel="next"` and `rel="prev"` format.
- `Idempotency-Key` header must be checked on POST /statuses to prevent duplicate posts.
- POST /api/v1/media must return a 202 with processing state if the image is not yet ready.
close_reason: Phanpy verified working — OAuth, timeline, posts all functional
